verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type I single-pass TM protein with an N-terminal extracellular domain. Canonical localization is ER-resident; however, it exhibits documented dual localization with a constitutive minority population at the plasma membrane (detected by surface biotinylation and immunofluorescence). The PM pool is achieved via its own TM topology and N-linked glycosylation-dependent trafficking (at Asn131 and Asn171), not transient recruitment. Absence of a di-lysine ER-retention motif permits partial escape to the PM. The protein does reach the outer leaflet by its own membrane anchor, but the majority-intracellular/minority-surface distribution pattern meets the contextual criterion.
